The pack's readiness claim is independently supported: descriptor-only implementation with validators that genuinely enforce no-write/no-real-data/no-leak/deny-over-allow, full plan↔manifest↔contract↔code↔test consistency, correct authority boundary (Claude read-only, not final approver; enterprise trust deferred to human approval; Hermes used only for deterministic evidence packets), and no over-claim of `production_ready` pre-closeout.","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"72151928-845c-4119-a51d-20628c105d44","total_cost_usd":3.1122370000000004,"usage":{"input_tokens":58,"cache_creation_input_tokens":124530,"cache_read_input_tokens":3231167,"output_tokens":28673,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":124530,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":2,"output_tokens":276,"cache_read_input_tokens":157039,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7000,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":7000},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"modelUsage":{"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001":{"inputTokens":1126,"outputTokens":20,"cacheReadInputTokens":0,"cacheCreationInputTokens":0,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":0.001226,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":32000},"claude-opus-4-8":{"inputTokens":58,"outputTokens":28673,"cacheReadInputTokens":3231167,"cacheCreationInputTokens":124530,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":3.1110110000000004,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":32000}},"permission_denials":[],"structured_output":{"overall_verdict":"PASS_WITH_FINDINGS","blocks_pack_closeout":false,"blocks_goal_closeout":false,"no_unresolved_p0":true,"no_unresolved_p1":true,"summary":"CP00-288 is a descriptor-only email/DMS permission slice (RP08.P06.M00.S07-RP08.P06.M07.S06, risk C, 150 units) whose pack-level readiness claim is supported by independent read-only inspection. The live closeout-pack-plan, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, contract projection, registry binding, service case-set/descriptor, validators, test suite, and contract validator are mutually consistent and free of drift: the CP288 binding matches the plan and baseline exactly (upstream CP00-287, next CP00-289 at RP08.P06.M07.S07, ledger SHA 13c7ed65), and deliverable/micro distributions equal the baseline (impl 56 / security_audit 35 / ui 28 / claude_review 7 / test 24 = 150). The validators genuinely enforce the claimed boundaries rather than asserting them cosmetically: freezeCp288Result plus CP288/CP287 row-extras set the exact fields validateEmailDmsCoreCp288PermissionSliceDescriptor checks (descriptor_only, no runtime_execution, deny_over_allow_enforced, permission_decision_detail_included:false, writes_audit_event:false, real_client_data_loaded:false, cross_tenant_access_allowed:false, leak_detected:false), and validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the committed contract against code constants. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold (command-evidence no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false; manifest implementation_summary affirms no runtime permission/audit/AI/object-storage/Hermes-receipt execution). Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval:false, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, human final approval required, Hermes (H08) used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets with no product runtime receipt. production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review is appropriately NOT over-claimed; the absent post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) reflect correct pre-review sequencing (pack_level_claude_review.status:pending). Review-hardening evidence is sound: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1454-byte valid JSON result), no CP00-288 invalid attempts recorded, and the unrelated LDIP invalid-attempt file is correctly scoped out. One P3 cosmetic metadata inconsistency in changed-file-scope.json with no gate impact. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack and goal closeout are not blocked.","findings":[{"id":"CP00-288-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"changed-file-scope.json dual-classifies pack evidence dir and handoff doc as both active-untracked and unrelated-preserved","file":"artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-288/changed-file-scope.json","line":100,"details":"docs/closeout-packs/cp00-288/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). These are contradictory labels: a file cannot be simultaneously an active untracked implementation file for this pack and an unrelated preserved file. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 5985-5988) uses the narrower, correct set (only the stray .docx and the unrelated LDIP invalid-attempt JSON), so the canonical scoping is unaffected. The dual-listing is confined to generated reviewer-scope metadata covering documentation/evidence files; it does not touch any product runtime artifact and has no effect on the production_ready determination, validation gates, or no-write/no-real-data boundaries.","recommendation":"Align changed-file-scope.json so the pack's own evidence directory (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-288/) and handoff doc are listed only as active pack artifacts, not under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, matching the manifest's worktree_transition_policy classification.
